sourceRef: tip:How to keep your queue readable, even at semester start

slides.headline: Manage your queue like a pro
slides.body: Get your school certifying queue ready for the semester.

slides.headline: Group by chapter
slides.body: Organize your queue by the chapter number of each student's GI Bill form.
bullets: This avoids clutter from forms with similar numbers

slides.headline: Prioritize by urgency
slides.body: Triage forms based on the urgency of each student's situation.
bullets: Immediate needs go first (e.g., Yellow Ribbon, priority enrollment)

slides.headline: Use templates for uniformity
slides.body: Create templates for common student situations.

slides.headline: Communicate clearly
slides.body: Inform students about their status and next steps.

slides.headline: Review and adjust
slides.body: Regularly review your queue and adjust your triage system.
Apply these tips to your queue management this semester.
slides.headline: Read the full post

outline.caption: VASCOs: Triage by chapter to keep your queue organized. Organize by chapter number, prioritize urgency, use templates, communicate clearly, and review regularly.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 4, which could benefit from a more compelling takeaway or a clearer visual element to draw attention.
